chunks.text: Matei Zaharia and John Duchi win ACM dissertation awards - EECS at Berkeley EECS alumni Matei Zaharia (advisor Profs. Scott Shenker & Ion Stoica ) and John Duchi (advisor Prof. Michael Jordan ) have won ACM (Assoc. for Computing Machinery) dissertation awards. Matei won the 2014 ACM Doctoral Dissertation Award for his thesis “An Architecture for Fast and General Data Processing on Large Clusters”, and fellow AMPLab alum John Duchi won Honorable Mention for his thesis “Multiple Optimality Guarantees in Statistical Learning”. Matei and John are now faculty members at MIT and Stanford, respectively. Categories Alumni Honors View Open Faculty Positions
